# Copilot instructions for this repository

## Build, test, and lint commands
- No build, test, or lint scripts are present in this repository.
- Quick preview: open `index.html` in a browser.
- Run a local static server to preview (optional):
  - Python: `python -m http.server 8000` (serve from repo root)
  - Node: `npx http-server .` or `npx serve .`

## High-level architecture
- Small static web app (no bundler or package manager present).
- Files of interest:
  - `index.html` — app structure and button markup. Buttons use `data-action` and `data-value` attributes to wire behavior.
  - `styles.css` — theming, layout, and responsive rules; uses CSS variables and utility classes for button styles.
  - `script.js` — all application logic in global scope: state variables, event wiring, calculation functions, and keyboard handlers.
- Runtime model: UI buttons and keyboard events call top-level handlers (handleDigit, handleOperator, etc.). State is held in module-level variables: `currentValue`, `storedValue`, `pendingOperator`, `waitingForNewValue`.

## Key conventions and patterns for Copilot
- UI wiring uses `data-action` and `data-value` attributes. When editing or adding buttons, preserve these attributes — JavaScript relies on them to dispatch actions.
- Keep logic inside `script.js` as plain functions (not assuming a module system). If refactoring, export or adapt callers consistently across `index.html` and `script.js`.
- Display formatting: `formatResult` limits precision; `calculate` returns the string `'Error'` on divide-by-zero — keep this sentinel value consistent.
- Button classes: `btn`, `operator`, `function`, `equals`, `wide` control styling/placement. Prefer these classes when adding UI elements.
- Accessibility: `role="application"`, `aria-live` on the display, and button grouping are present. Preserve or improve ARIA attributes when changing UI.

## Editing guidance for assistants
- Don’t assume a Node toolchain or tests exist. Avoid adding tooling without updating repository docs.
- For UI changes, update `index.html`, `styles.css`, and `script.js` together; keep the `data-*` attributes and class names in-sync.
- For logic changes, maintain global state semantics or migrate all consumers in one change to avoid inconsistent behavior.

## Other repository files
- No CONTRIBUTING.md, AGENTS.md, or other assistant config files were found. If adding CI or test harness, include clear commands in this file.


## Playwright (end-to-end) setup
- A minimal Playwright configuration has been added to support end-to-end browser tests.
- Files added:
  - `package.json` — scripts: `start` (static server) and `test:e2e` (runs Playwright).
  - `playwright.config.js` — starts a local `python -m http.server` on port 8000 before tests.
  - `tests/calculator.spec.js` — example E2E test that verifies a simple addition.

How to run E2E tests locally:
1. Install dependencies: `npm install` (installs `@playwright/test`).
2. Run tests: `npm run test:e2e` (Playwright will start a local server then run tests).
